title: Getting Started with Plotscape
text: Install latest development version plotscaper using devtools package:

what: Minimal Example: A Linked Barplot and Scatterplot
title: Getting Started with Plotscape
text: first step making plotscaper visualization creating visual scene. can think scene box hold interactive plots. things going background, ’s important right now. create visual scene, call ps_scene() function one arguments. first argument always supplied data, needs R data.frame (tibble). Let’s use mtcars data available base R: can see right now grey rectangle small help button (marked ?) top right corner. actually leverage power plotscaper, need add plots. add plots plotscaper scene, call functions ps_[plot type]() syntax. example, add scatterplot use ps_scatter(), add histogram use ps_histo(), . case, argument always supplied mapping. Mapping specifies variables data get mapped onto aesthetics within plot. feel familiar ggplot2 users. specify mapping, use ps_map() function names variables data arguments. can chain adding multiple plots together using native pive (|>) operator (%*% magrittr package works ). example, add scatterplot weight (wt) mileage (mpg), barplot number cylinders (cyl), can : scene now contains scatterplot barplot, laid side--side! ’s everything. Try clicking one points bars select . see object (point bar) becomes highlighted, indicated vibrant color. ’s , also see plot respond highlighting ’s object(s), corresponding cases data. many ways can interact plotscaper scenes. useful commands : Click plot make active Double-click deactivate plots clear selections +/- increase/decrease size objects (active) plot [/] decrease/increase opacity objects Click object transiently select , click--drag select one object Hold 1, 2, … clicking (clicking--dragging) make permanent (group) selection might wondering terms transient permanent mentioned mean. Put simply, represent two different modes selecting objects. Transient selection default. click--drag select objects without holding additional keys, objects transiently selected. Clicking somewhere else plot clear transient selection. Conversely, holding either “1”, “2”, “3” key clicking--dragging lead objects permanently selected. Permanently selected objects highlighted different color, selection get cleared single click (however, double-clicking scene remove selections). ’s , least one case data permanently selected, transient selection change behavior: instead highlighting objects solid color, now overlay striped pattern. Using transient permanent selection together useful comparing within groups. example, can assign heavy inefficient cars (mileage lower 20 miles per gallon, weigh 3,000 pounds, lower right quadrant scatterplot) group 1, cars group 2, check 6-cylinder cars belong either group transiently selecting relevant bar barplot. want practice selections manually, go ahead try plot . ’re just big picture, take look plot , showcases another handy plotscaper function: ps_premark(). ps_premark() can used initialize scene selections already present upon rendering, like : Simultaneously using transient selection allows us see 6-cylinder cars belong heavy--inefficient light--efficient category. , plot illustrates can use ps_premark() mark scene highlighted selections user even touches . Importantly, scene remains regular interactive plotscaper scene selections can cleared double-click. , ps_premark() can useful want show someone else interesting features data also allow freedom venture ! played around barplot scatterplot . adding plots types mix? Histograms time-proven classic, “squareplots” (let know know better name) handy allow us easily visualize two categorical variables . Interactive graphics can really fun efficient way discovering interesting trends patterns data. example, look top-left scatterplot scene : playing around mtcars dataset years, testing different models ways visualizing data. However, never noticed look weight mileage 5-gear automatic cars, lie main diagonal. means cars less efficient expect cars equivalent weight. come kind insight using regular static plots take either lot time knowing look . plotscaper, can generate insights fly!
code: library(plotscaper)  scene1 <- ps_scene(data = mtcars)  scene1 scene1 <- scene1 |>    ps_scatter(mapping = ps_map(x = "wt", y = "mpg")) |>   ps_bar(mapping = ps_map(x = "cyl"))  scene1 heavy_inefficient_cars <- which(mtcars$wt > 3 & mtcars$mpg < 20) light_efficient_cars <- setdiff(1:32, heavy_inefficient_cars) six_cylinder_cars <- which(mtcars$cyl == 6)  scene1 |>    ps_premark(heavy_inefficient_cars, 2) |>   ps_premark(light_efficient_cars, 3) |>   ps_premark(six_cylinder_cars, 128) scene1 <- scene1 |>   ps_histo(ps_map(x = "disp")) |>   ps_square(ps_map("gear", "am"))  scene1 five_gear_automatic <-  which(mtcars$am == 1 & mtcars$gear == 5)  scene1 |>   ps_premark(five_gear_automatic, 128)

what: Getting More Creative with Layouts and Adding Plots Programmatically
title: Getting Started with Plotscape
text: two powerful tools creating interesting plotscaper scenes: CSS grid layout leveraging R add plots existing scene dynamically, using code.

previous_headings: Getting More Creative with Layouts and Adding Plots Programmatically
title: Getting Started with Plotscape
text: CSS grid layout can used define size, position, aspect ratio plots within scene. specified using matrix numbers (integers), number represents plot, order get added scene. example, supply following layout: end short--wide first plot occupying top row, tall--narrow second plot occupying rightmost column, larger square third plot occupying bottom left corner, fourth fifth plots squeezed . actually tell plotscaper layout want use, need specify using R’s matrix() function. restriction plots need arranged contiguous rectangles, otherwise scene rendered. example, following layout render: one :

previous_headings: Getting More Creative with Layouts and Adding Plots Programmatically
what: Leveraging R to add plots for us
title: Getting Started with Plotscape
text: Since functions add plots plotscaper scene regular R functions, can write R code specify plots want add. Specifically, iteration using loops sapply()/lapply() can handy want add multiple plots don’t want write every single call ps_[plot type]() manually. example, want plot multiple scatterplots, names variables want plot x- y-axis stored string vectors xvars yvars respectively, can something like: Putting together, ’s can create interesting scene iris dataset:
code: scene <- ...  for (i in seq_along(xvars)) {   scene <- scene |> ps_scatter(ps_map(xvars[i], yvars[i])) } # Create layout:  # - 6 small scatterplot in top-left corner # - 4 histograms in the right column # - One large barplot in bottom-left corner layout2 <- matrix(c(   1, 2, 3, 8, 8,   4, 5, 6, 9, 9,   7, 7, 7, 10, 10,   7, 7, 7, 11, 11), ncol = 5, byrow = TRUE)  scene2 <- ps_scene(iris, layout = layout2)  # Create a matrix of all unique combinations of 2 variables in the iris data vars <- names(iris) xyvars <- matrix(vars[combn(1:4, 2)], ncol = 2, byrow = TRUE)  # Add scatterplots for (i in seq_along(xyvars[, 1])) {   scene2 <- scene2 |> ps_scatter(ps_map(xyvars[i, 1], xyvars[i, 2])) }  # Add barplot scene2 <- scene2 |> ps_bar(ps_map("Species"))  # Add histograms for (i in seq_along(names(iris)[-5])) {   scene2 <- scene2 |> ps_histo(ps_map(names(iris)[i])) }  scene2

title: Shiny bindings for plotscaper — plotscaper-shiny
text: Output render functions using plotscaper within Shiny applications interactive Rmd documents.

title: Shiny bindings for plotscaper — plotscaper-shiny
code: plotscaperOutput(outputId, width = "100%", height = "400px")  renderPlotscaper(expr, env = parent.frame(), quoted = FALSE)

title: Shiny bindings for plotscaper — plotscaper-shiny
text: outputId output variable read width, height Must valid CSS unit (like '100%', '400px', 'auto') number, coerced string 'px' appended. expr expression generates plotscaper env environment evaluate expr. quoted expr quoted expression (quote())? useful want save expression variable.

title: Add a ready-made plot to a plotscaper scene — ps_wrapper_plot
code: s1 <- scene(mtcars, 900, 500) #> Error in scene(mtcars, 900, 500): could not find function "scene" s1 |> ps_wrapper_plot(type = "scater", mapping = list(x = "wt", y = "mpg")) #> Error in ps_wrapper_plot(s1, type = "scater", mapping = list(x = "wt",     y = "mpg")): object 's1' not found
